author: y4rr
content: Follow-up to Phase 11 (#1013) Swin-T rebuild. Three primitives currently break training-mode gradient flow but not eval-mode value-parity. (1) ferrotorch-core/src/ops/tensor_ops.rs:167 roll<T> reconstructs Tensor::from_storage with requires_grad=false — no grad_fn registered. Required for cyclic-shift backward in ShiftedWindowAttention. (2) Differentiable index_select / gather over a 2-D table: ShiftedWindowAttention::build_relative_position_bias does a CPU data_vec gather of relative_position_bias_table by relative_position_index. Eval-mode flows through fine; training requires gradient flow back into the bias table. (3) Differentiable strided-slice + cat for PatchMerging::forward (the four-corner concat is currently CPU data_vec). For full Swin-T training, all three must be migrated to autograd-tracked primitives. None of these affect the eval-mode value-parity test which is the Phase 11 target.
